name: aromatic_backbones_raise_tg
description: Aromatic backbones tend to raise Tg.

name: multi_aromatic_rigidity
description: Multiple aromatic rings increase chain rigidity.

name: imide_anhydride_networks_raise_tg
description: Imide or anhydride-derived networks often provide high Tg.

name: cyanate_ester_triazine
description: Cyanate ester triazine networks can be high Tg.

name: maleimide_rigid_network
description: Bismaleimide motifs can create rigid high-Tg networks.

name: nitrile_rich_rigidity
description: Nitrile-rich aromatic monomers often stiffen networks.

name: sulfone_diamine_rigidity
description: Sulfone-linked aromatic diamines are common high-Tg hard segments.

name: high_functionality_crosslink_density
description: Higher reactive functionality can increase crosslink density.

name: flexible_ether_penalty
description: Long flexible ether segments can lower Tg.

name: peg_like_penalty
description: PEG-like segments are a strong low-Tg risk.

name: long_aliphatic_penalty
description: Long aliphatic segments usually lower Tg.

name: peptide_like_out_of_domain
description: Peptide-like ChEMBL molecules are poor monomer hypotheses.

name: too_flexible_penalty
description: High rotatable-bond count increases flexibility risk.

name: heavy_halogen_practical_risk
description: Iodinated or brominated drug-like structures are lower-priority monomer hypotheses.

name: stereochemical_complexity_penalty
description: Many stereocenters often indicate bioactive-molecule complexity rather than monomer suitability.

name: druglike_hetero_complexity_penalty
description: High HBA/HBD complexity is a risk for out-of-library monomer transfer.

name: formal_charge_practical_penalty
description: Charged molecules are lower-priority thermoset monomer hypotheses unless specifically justified.

name: observed_chembl_transferable_monomers
description: Observed ChEMBL molecules passing hard validators and producing near-target predictions.

name: observed_three_or_more_component_blending
description: Observed near-target candidates where n>=3 tunes Tg toward the target.
